chapters.title: Just Enough Complex Analysis
chapters.notes: Explicitly builds on Ch1 formal power series becoming complex functions; references Ch1 generalized binomial theorem for branch-point coefficient extraction; previews transfer theorem of Ch4 and symbolic method of Ch3.

chapters.title: The Symbolic Method
chapters.notes: Requires Ch1 for OGF/EGF definitions and convolution. References Ch2 for convergence/analyticity (optional — the symbolic method itself is purely algebraic). Previews asymptotic results of Ch4 and Ch5. The complex-analytic content of Ch2 is not used in the proofs of Ch3.

chapters.title: Singularity Analysis
key_concepts_introduced: catalogue of singular exponents (poles, square-root, inverse square-root, log)
chapters.notes: Explicitly uses Cauchy integral formula and contour deformation from Ch2 in the proof sketch. Requires Ch3's symbolic method to set up GF equations whose singularities are analyzed. Applies the transfer theorem to the Catalan GF derived in Ch1.

chapters.title: Algebraic Generating Functions and the n^{-3/2} Law
chapters.notes: Directly applies the Flajolet-Odlyzko transfer theorem from Ch4 as the main tool. Symbolic method from Ch3 is used in examples but not strictly required to follow the main theorem chain. The Catalan/plane tree examples re-use Ch1 and Ch3 results.

chapters.title: Formal Languages and the Chomsky Hierarchy
chapters.notes: Requires Ch5 for the Chomsky-Schutzenberger connection (algebraic GF for CFLs) stated as a theorem. Symbolic method (Ch3) is referenced but the chapter is largely self-contained in its automata-theory content. The analytic payoff section ties back to Ch4-5.

chapters.title: Weighted Finite Automata and Rational Power Series
chapters.notes: Requires Ch4 for the partial-fraction / singularity analysis of the rational GF that emerges. Requires Ch6 for the formal-language context (DFAs, recognizable series, Kleene's theorem). Also uses Perron-Frobenius, previewing Ch9.

chapters.title: Probabilistic Grammars and Tightness
chapters.notes: Requires Ch5 for PCFGs and algebraic GFs (the PGF framework for nonterminals). Requires Ch7 for the WFA/stochastic-automaton context and the stochastic-normalization discussion. The Galton-Watson branching process theory used is self-contained.

chapters.title: Entropy Rate and the Identity h = log(1/R)
key_concepts_introduced: counting GF vs length PGF distinction
entropy rate of natural language estimates
chapters.notes: Requires Ch2 for Cauchy-Hadamard (used to derive h = log(1/R) from the radius of convergence). Requires Ch8 for the tightness prerequisite (P(1)=1, ensuring the length PGF is a valid probability distribution). References Ch7 for finite-state sources and transfer matrices (optional, as context). References Ch4 transfer theorem for singularity-type / convergence-rate discussion.

chapters.title: Autoregressive Language Models
chapters.notes: Requires Ch8 for tightness of Transformers (the Du et al. theorem is restated and applied as the central permission for the rest of the book). Requires Ch9 for the distinction between length PGF and counting GF, which is the conceptual framework of the whole chapter. References Ch7 for the Markov-chain / rational-GF perspective.

chapters.title: Formal Language Theory of Transformers
key_concepts_introduced: AC^0, TC^0, NC^1 circuit complexity classes
Rizvi et al. Transformer simulates WFA
chapters.notes: Requires Ch7 for WFAs and the rational-GF framework (the simulation theorems are about WFA simulation). Requires Ch10 for the LLM distribution framework being analyzed. References Ch5 for algebraic GFs and the CoT/PCFG connection. References Ch6 for formal language hierarchy context.

chapters.title: Approximating Language Models by Weighted Finite Automata
key_concepts_introduced: Hankel matrix of a string function
chapters.notes: Requires Ch7 for the WFA rationality theorem and the GF framework F(z) = alpha(I-zA)^{-1}beta. Requires Ch11 for the architectural context (why approximation is needed, TC^0 ceiling). References Ch9 for the entropy-rate identity h=log(1/R) applied to the WFA approximation. References Ch4 for singularity analysis of the resulting rational GF.

chapters.title: Boltzmann Sampling
key_concepts_introduced: recursive Boltzmann construction (union, product, SEQ, SET, CYC)
chapters.notes: Requires Ch3 (symbolic method) for the recursive construction from combinatorial specifications. Requires Ch4 (singularity analysis / transfer theorem) for the coefficient asymptotics used in the rejection-sampling complexity analysis and the n^{-3/2} / critical-temperature discussion. The GF = partition function identification is the chapter's core.

chapters.title: Gibbs Measures and Temperature Decoding
key_concepts_introduced: top-k and nucleus sampling as non-Gibbs truncations
chapters.notes: Requires Ch13 for the Boltzmann/Gibbs/partition-function framework (directly continued here). Requires Ch10 for the autoregressive LLM definition and the energy factorization. References Ch8 for tightness (needed for the global normalization). The ARM-EBM bijection builds on the tightness framework.

chapters.title: Phase Transitions in Language Model Output
key_concepts_introduced: composition schemes and universal transition signatures
empirical evidence (Nakaishi et al., Arnold et al., Mikhaylovskiy)
bridging proposal (LLM transitions as analytic phase transitions)
chapters.notes: Requires Ch4 for the singularity classification (pole vs branch point => transition order). Requires Ch14 for the partition function Z(beta) and Gibbs framework applied to LLMs. References Ch5 for the n^{-3/2} universality and PCFG/algebraic connection. References Banderier-Kuba-Wallner (2021) composition scheme results as the theoretical framework.

chapters.title: Zipf's Law and Token Distributions
key_concepts_introduced: Mikhaylovskiy's temperature window and Zipf exponent
bivariate GF proposal for joint rank-length distribution
chapters.notes: Requires Ch4 for the singularity analysis and bivariate GF transfer lemma used in the BGF proposal. Requires Ch15 for the temperature window context (Mikhaylovskiy's result directly connects phase transitions to Zipf behavior). References Ch3 for bivariate GF machinery. The chapter is largely review/proposal-oriented.

chapters.title: Motif Statistics and Analytic Information Theory
key_concepts_introduced: Gaussian limit law for motif counts (Nicodeme-Salvy-Flajolet)
LLM motif statistics via WFA approximation
chapters.notes: Requires Ch4 for singularity analysis / transfer lemma (the singularity perturbation argument for Gaussian limits). Requires Ch7 for WFAs and the transfer matrix framework (building the product automaton for motif counting). Requires Ch9 for the entropy rate identity h=log(1/R) (used in trie depth connection and the WFA entropy rate). References Ch12 for WFA approximation context (optional — needed only for the LLM application discussion).

chapters.title: Ten Open Problems at the Frontier
key_concepts_introduced: Gap 1b (entropy-rate identity for LLM counting GF)
Gap 5 (Gaussian limits via Hwang quasi-power theorem)
Gap 9 (WFA pipeline with analytic error bounds)
chapters.notes: This is a synthesis chapter that references essentially all prior chapters. The most directly essential are Ch10 (LLM framework), Ch12 (WFA approximation), Ch15 (phase transitions), and Ch17 (analytic information theory), as each Gap is explicitly tied to gaps in those chapters' stories. Also requires Ch9 for Gap 1b and Ch5 for Gap 4.

chapters.title: A Research Roadmap
key_concepts_introduced: tractability ranking of the ten gaps
recommended reading order for new researchers
chapters.notes: This chapter is purely a synthesis and roadmap chapter. It requires Ch18 (the ten gaps) as its direct prerequisite, since it is organized around prioritizing and sequencing those gaps. All other references are backward pointers to the full book.
